$comment: Machine-readable layout + component contract for slidev-theme-tahta. Source of truth — AGENTS.md is generated from this (npm run docs). Consumed via slidev-theme-tahta/layouts.json.

themeConfig.description: Deck-level config set in the first slide's headmatter under themeConfig.

fields.name: variant
fields.description: Choose one deliberately to fit the talk's tone/audience (see the Variants table's 'best for'). Don't omit it to coast on a default.

fields.name: accent
fields.description: Override the brand accent (any CSS color). The whole palette (tints, shades, chart series) derives from it.

fields.name: lang
fields.description: BCP-47 language tag, e.g. 'tr'. Drives correct locale casing on uppercase kickers (Turkish i→İ).

universal.description: Per-slide frontmatter available on every layout.

fields.name: bg
fields.description: Slide background. A generated (asset-free, accent-derived) background — mesh | aurora | grain | dots | grid — or an image path/URL, which is painted under an automatic contrast scrim. Opt-in; omit for the variant's default.

fields.name: ghost
fields.description: Giant faint background glyph (on default/section/stats/steps/fact).

fields.name: glow
fields.description: Force the accent glow on (topic slides) or off (cover/section/statement).

fields.name: foot
fields.description: Override the auto footer label for this slide.

rules: Pick the layout that matches the content shape; fill its frontmatter fields. Do not write CSS, grids, or layout HTML.
Titles/subtitles may contain highlight (accent color) or highlight (italic accent — the editorial emphasis). Nothing else needs HTML.

For varied, designed decks vary the composition: open with lead, punctuate with bigtype, and use metric/agenda/define/columns/panels/reference/vs for teaching content — not every slide as a centered title+body.

Choose a variant deliberately. themeConfig.variant is required — pick the one whose 'best for' fits the talk's tone, audience, and medium (see the Variants table); never omit it to coast on a default. When you hand back the deck, tell the user which variant you chose and why, and that they can switch it — the visual direction is a shared decision.

Layouts are your default — the design is built in, so reach for the one that matches the content shape (definition→define, comparison→vs, numbers→stats, process→steps). But don't force every slide into a preset: default/two-cols bodies (and the space under a statement) are a canvas — compose components there (<Callout>, <Stat>, <Plot>, <Terminal>, <Kbd>, <Figure>, <Meter>, <Tags>, …) to enrich a slide, and reach for them whenever no layout cleanly fits. A deck that mixes designed layouts with a few composed slides reads richer than one that only fills templates.

Use components liberally AND with variety — they are the main source of richness and make each deck feel bespoke rather than templated. Map content to the component it invites: a number→<Stat>/<Meter>, a status→<Badge>, a stack/skill list→<Tags>, a person→<Person>, a command/artifact→<Terminal>/<Plot>/<Kbd>, an aside→<Callout>. Aim for a component on most content slides, and reach for several different ones across the deck — leaning on a single type (e.g. only <Callout>) still reads templated. A deck where nearly every slide carries a fitting component reads custom; one that only fills layout fields reads generic.

Slides are separated by a single ---. Don't add an extra --- after a slide's body — the next slide's frontmatter --- already separates them; a --- immediately followed by another --- renders a blank slide.

The opening --- block is both the deck headmatter and the first slide's frontmatter — don't set the same key twice in it. A deck-level title plus the first slide's title collide (duplicate YAML key) and break slidev export; let the cover's title stand as the deck title, or start with a headmatter-only block and put the cover next.

Before finishing, validate the deck: run `npx tahta-lint slides.md` (ships with the theme) and fix what it reports — it catches empty slides, unclosed frontmatter, missing required fields, and bad enum values.
class: dropcap on a default slide sets a drop cap on the first paragraph.
Footer label auto-fills from the deck title (override per slide with foot:). Never add page numbers.
Keep numeric values bare; put the symbol in unit (value: 80, unit: "%").
For cover/section/statement/end/fact, the title comes from frontmatter — leave the slide body empty.
Inside { ... } flow rows, quote any value containing a comma or colon (before: "$4,200").
ghost: (on default/section/stats/steps/fact) prints a faint giant background glyph.
Entrance motion is automatic, themeable per variant, and disabled in print + reduced-motion.

fields.name: title
fields.description: HTML allowed (accent2 span).

layouts.example: layout: cover kicker: Team · 2026 title: Shipping Faster subtitle: How we cut p95 80%

fields.name: index
fields.description: Big faint background glyph + small accent label.

layouts.example:
layout: section
index: "02"
kicker: Part two
title: The migration

layouts.useFor: General content; body is markdown (bullets, prose). Auto fit-to-frame. A canvas — compose components in the body for richer slides.
layouts.example: layout: default kicker: The problem title: Slow far from us-east-1 --- - Average round trip was 220ms - p95 hit 480ms

layout: statement
kicker: The setup
title: Your dashboard says 42ms. Your angriest user waited 1.9s.

layout: quote
quote: It just feels instant now.
author: A beta user

layout: fact
kicker: The takeaway
value: "80"
unit: "%"
label: lower p95 worldwide

fields.name: columns
fields.description: Header labels; default [Metric, Before, After, Δ].

layouts.example:
layout: compare
kicker: Before vs after
title: Every metric moved
rows:
  - { metric: p95, before: 480ms, after: 95ms, delta: −80% }

layout: chart
kicker: Before vs after
title: Latency, drawn
chart:
  type: area
  unit: ms
  categories: [p50, p95, cold start]
  series:
    - { name: Before, data: [120, 480, 900] }
    - { name: After, data: [38, 95, 12] }

layout: steps
kicker: The migration
title: Incremental, zero downtime
steps:
  - { title: Extract, desc: pull handlers out, icon: "lucide:scissors" }

layout: feature
kicker: Why it works
title: Three reasons
features:
  - { icon: "lucide:zap", title: Fast, desc: Edge in 300+ locations }

layout: timeline
kicker: Roadmap
title: Next quarters
events:
  - { date: Q3, title: Alerts, desc: anomaly detection }

layout: logos
kicker: Trusted by
title: Teams on the edge
logos:
  - { icon: "lucide:cloud", text: Northwind }

layouts.useFor: Code, optionally Magic Move. Body is a fenced code block.
layouts.example:
layout: code
kicker: How it works
title: Cache, then render
---

````md magic-move
```js
const x = await get(url)
```
```js
const x = await cache.match(url) ?? get(url)
```
````

layouts.useFor: Generic split; left = body, right = after ::right::. Each column is a canvas — compose components in either.
layouts.example:
layout: two-cols
title: Before vs after
---

**Before** — one region.

::right::

**After** — 18 regions.

layouts.useFor: Text + a side image (markdown body is the text column).

fields.name: image
fields.description: Path in the deck's public/ folder, e.g. /shot.png.

layouts.example:
layout: image
side: right
image: /shot.png
title: The product
---

- A media column plus a text column
layout: showcase side: right image: /shot.png kicker: The product title: Finally visible subtitle: One line of config.

layouts.useFor: Full-bleed image hero with overlaid text.

fields.name: stat
fields.description: A giant overlaid figure.

fields.name: duotone
fields.description: Grayscale + accent duotone the image (default true).

layouts.example:
layout: bleed
image: /cover.jpg
duotone: true
kicker: Results
stat: "−80%"
title: p95 latency, worldwide

fields.name: video
fields.description: mp4 URL/path.

fields.name: iframe
fields.description: Embed URL (https).

layouts.example:
layout: embed
title: Demo
video: /demo.mp4

layout: end
title: Thanks
subtitle: Questions welcome
contact: team@example.com

layouts.useFor: Asymmetric opener — title anchored low-left, big negative space. A dramatic alternative to cover.

fields.name: title
fields.description: HTML allowed: word = italic accent emphasis; word = accent color.

fields.name: index
fields.description: Big faint ghost glyph in the negative space.

layouts.example: layout: lead index: "01" kicker: SREcon · 2026 title: The tail that wags the service.

layouts.useFor: Full-bleed type — one phrase fills the slide. A punctuation/transition moment; auto-fits to the frame.

fields.name: title
fields.description: HTML allowed (em / accent2).

layouts.example: layout: bigtype kicker: The takeaway title: Optimize the number your worst customer feels.

layouts.useFor: Asymmetric single metric — giant number on one side, context on the other (vs the centered `fact`).

fields.name: value
fields.description: Keep the number bare; put the symbol in unit.

fields.name: label
fields.description: Context line; HTML allowed.

layouts.example: layout: metric kicker: The arithmetic of scale value: "63" unit: "%" label: of renders hit at least one p99 call.

layouts.useFor: Numbered module overview / table of contents.

fields.name: definition
fields.description: HTML allowed (accent2 span).

fields.name: points
fields.description: Supporting bullets (string array).

layouts.example: layout: define kicker: The basics term: What is Claude Code? definition: An agentic coding assistant in your terminal.

layouts.useFor: 2–3 headed columns side by side (compare / parallel lists).
Cheatsheet — term → description pairs, optionally grouped. For commands, flags, config keys, shortcuts.

fields.name: groups
fields.description: Grouped sections. Use groups OR items.

fields.name: items
fields.description: Flat list (no grouping).

layouts.example:
layout: reference
title: Slash commands
groups:
  - { title: Essentials, items: [{ term: "/help", desc: list commands }, { term: "/clear", desc: reset }] }

layouts.useFor: A-vs-B comparison — two panels with a centered divider.

fields.name: label
fields.description: Divider label (default "vs").

layouts.useFor: Code (in the slide body) + numbered explanation beside it.

fields.name: notes
fields.description: Numbered notes shown beside the code (string array); HTML allowed (strong).

layouts.example: layout: code-explain title: Auto-format on edit notes: - "PostToolUse fires after a tool runs." - "Match Edit|Write to scope to file edits." --- ```json { "hooks": {} } ```

components.name: Stat
components.example: <Stat value="80" unit="%" label="lower p95" tone="good" icon="lucide:trending-down" />

components.name: Plot
components.useFor: ECharts chart (alias: BarChart). Used by the chart layout.

components.name: Callout
components.example: <Callout tone="warn" icon="lucide:triangle-alert">Heads up: this is the one risk.</Callout>

components.name: Reveal
components.useFor: Themeable entrance wrapper for extra body content.

components.name: Fit
components.useFor: Auto fit-to-frame: scales overflowing content down.
components.example: <Fit>long body content that should never overflow</Fit>

components.name: Figure
components.useFor: Image (or slotted diagram) with caption + optional credit.
components.example: <Figure src="/dashboard.png" caption="The tail, finally visible" credit="v2 UI" />

components.name: Meter
components.example: <Meter value="72" label="Migration" /> · <Meter value="3" max="5" display="3 / 5" tone="warn" label="Hardening" />

components.name: Person
components.useFor: Avatar + name + role (bios, team, attribution). Falls back to initials with no photo.

components.name: Tags
components.useFor: Row of keyword chips (tech stack, topics, skills).
